root_cause.text: Query string parameters containing `<script>` tags were not properly sanitized or encoded before being included in generated URLs, leading to a potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds explicit logic to `UrlHelper.java` to check for the presence of `<script>` tags (`result.indexOf("<script>") >= 0`) and replaces them with the harmless string `script` before the URL is finalized.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: Query string parameters containing `<script>` tags were not properly sanitized or encoded before being included in generated URLs, leading to a potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds explicit logic to `UrlHelper.java` to check for the presence of `<script>` tags (`result.indexOf("<script>") >= 0`) and replaces them with the harmless string `script` before the URL is finalized.

intent_record.intent_summary: Sanitize generated URLs by removing literal '<script>' tags to mitigate potential XSS vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: Added a string replacement step to strip '<script>' tags from the URL string before it is optionally encoded.
intent_record.how: Prevents literal '<script>' tags from being included in the final URL output, providing a rudimentary defense against cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

grouping_result.intent: Sanitize generated URLs by removing literal '<script>' tags to mitigate potential XSS vulnerabilities.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by sanitizing generated URLs and removing literal '<script>' tags to prevent XSS.

candidates.rationale: This statement generates the final URL string from the link object (which contains the parameters) without sanitizing or properly encoding the <script> tags, leading to the XSS vulnerability.
This statement appends the query string parameters to the URL link without ensuring they are sanitized against <script> tags.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement generates the final URL string from the link object (which contains the parameters) without sanitizing or properly encoding the <script> tags, leading to the XSS vulnerability.
This statement appends the query string parameters to the URL link without ensuring they are sanitized against <script> tags.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Initial import of WebWork 2, version 2.2.2


git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/incubator/webwork2@388283 13f7
bic_trace_step.reason: In the `buildParametersString` method, the parameter keys (`name`) are appended directly to the generated URL at line 179 (`link.append(name);`) without being passed through `translateAndEncode` or any URL encoding/sanitization functions. This allows malicious strings like `<script>` tags in parameter names to be reflected unescaped.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
WW-1938 Bug with multiple s:param tags inside s:url tag

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/struts/struts2/tru
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit ffd0fb693bd31f0fb1a7dd8981cea78637e40b6d
Author: James Holmes <jholmes@apache.org>
Date:   Tue Jul 24 17:33:38 2007 +0000

    WW-1938 Bug with multiple s:param tags inside s:url tag
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/struts/struts2/trunk@559127 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/org/apache/struts2/views/util/UrlHelper.java b/core/src/main/java/org/apache/struts2/views/util/UrlHelper.java
index ea80935f0..708bb838e 100644
--- a/core/sr
bic_trace_step.reason: In the `buildParameterSubstring` method (lines 247-254), the parameter `name` is appended to the StringBuilder without being encoded (`builder.append(name);`), while the value is encoded (`builder.append(translateAndEncode(value));`). This allows unencoded characters like `<script>` tags in parameter names to be included in generated URLs, causing the XSS vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Initial import of WebWork 2, version 2.2.2


git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/incubator/webwork2@388283 13f7
bic_trace_step.reason: In `buildParametersString`, the parameter key (`name`) is appended directly to the `link` buffer without any URL encoding or sanitization (line 179: `link.append(name);`), whereas only the value is encoded via `translateAndEncode(values[i])`. This allows arbitrary injected content such as `<script>` tags in parameter names to be reflected in generated URLs.
